notes: This artifact exists to prevent implementation drift from the Vouch source of truth.
Vouch is deterministic commitment-backed payment coordination.
Stripe owns payment truth; Vouch owns workflow truth.
No discretionary settlement, marketplace, messaging, review, rating, search, browse, dispute, appeal, evidence, or mediation behavior may be introduced.

findings.source_rule: Vouch is a narrow commitment-backed payment coordination system for appointments and in-person agreements.
findings.required_action: Preserve scope. Do not expand into marketplace, scheduling, discovery, messaging, reviews, ratings, dispute resolution, arbitration, or escrow behavior.

findings.source_rule: Both participants confirm presence inside the confirmation window before funds release.
findings.required_action: Settlement capture may occur only after bilateral confirmation and provider-confirmed capturable state.

findings.source_rule: If bilateral confirmation conditions fail, funds do not release.
findings.required_action: Use provider-appropriate non-capture, cancel, void, expiration, or refund behavior based on current Stripe state.

findings.source_rule: Stripe owns payment truth; Vouch owns workflow truth.
findings.required_action: Retrieve Stripe provider state before all capture, cancel, void, refund, and retry operations.

findings.source_rule: Vouch does not ask who is right. Vouch asks what happened.
findings.required_action: Do not implement disputes, appeals, screenshots, evidence review, mediation, arbitration, manual awards, support overrides, or confirmation rewrites.

findings.source_rule: The user-facing app surface is intentionally narrow.
findings.required_action: Allowed tenant routes are dashboard, vouches/new, vouches/new/confirm, and vouches/[vouchId]. Do not add setup, settings, admin, messages, disputes, evidence, reviews, ratings, search, browse, providers, or marketplace routes.

findings.source_rule: Canonical Vouch lifecycle is draft, committed, sent, accepted, authorized, confirmable, completed, expired.
findings.required_action: Keep payment, provider, settlement, archive, webhook, and recovery states on separate axes.

findings.source_rule: Canonical confirmation methods are code_exchange and offline_code_exchange.
findings.required_action: Reject GPS, manual, system, screenshot, evidence, support, and subjective confirmation methods.

findings.source_rule: Protected reads go through fetchers. Protected writes go through server actions.
findings.required_action: Route files remain thin. No Prisma, Stripe SDK, DTO shaping, authz enforcement, workflow mutation, or provider mutation belongs in route shells or pure UI components.

findings.source_rule: Webhook processing is idempotent and provider-event deduped.
findings.required_action: Record every provider event ID once. Duplicate events must acknowledge without rerunning transitions. Late events apply only valid forward movement.

findings.source_rule: Readiness gates are embedded in canonical flows.
findings.required_action: Keep payment readiness, payout readiness, terms acceptance, and verification readiness as internal gates, not standalone setup/settings products.

findings.source_rule: Operational retry may exist only for deterministic technical recovery.
findings.required_action: No admin arbitration, manual payout, force release, manual refund decision, evidence review, or confirmation rewrite may exist.

findings.source_rule: Notifications and analytics may exist only as system-generated operational/product instrumentation.
findings.required_action: Do not allow notifications to become messaging, negotiation, dispute communication, or evidence channels.

findings.source_rule: Generated Prisma artifacts must not be manually edited.
findings.required_action: Edit prisma/schema.prisma only. Regenerate generated artifacts through Prisma commands.

findings.source_rule: Validation must catch contract drift and forbidden surface reintroduction.
findings.required_action: Run prisma, contract, lint, typecheck, unit, e2e, validate, and validate:all gates before marking launch-ready.

allowed_with_constraints.constraint: Allowed only as internal deterministic inspection/retry tooling, never user-facing arbitration or settlement control.
Allowed only as system-generated operational notification, never chat or negotiation.
Allowed only as product instrumentation without sensitive provider, identity, dispute, or meeting-purpose data.
Allowed only as payment aliases where customer maps to payer and merchant maps to payee.
